task: Create a structured review plan for https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com. Fetch the homepage and identify key pages and areas to audit. This is a financial services website so pay special attention to trust signals, compliance language, fiduciary disclosures, and regulatory requirements. Organize into categories: UX/Design, Performance, Security, SEO, Accessibility, Content Quality, and Financial Compliance. Output a prioritized checklist.

Audit https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com for UX/UI quality. Fetch the homepage and key pages. Analyze: visual hierarchy, navigation flow, responsive design indicators, component consistency, typography, color usage, call-to-action effectiveness, trust signals (testimonials, certifications, security badges), and overall user experience for a financial services audience. Provide severity-rated findings (P0-P3).

Analyze https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com for accessibility compliance. Fetch the homepage and check WCAG 2.1 AA conformance: semantic HTML structure, ARIA usage, keyboard navigation indicators, color contrast, focus management, alt text on images, form labels, and screen reader compatibility. Financial services sites must be ADA-compliant. Provide specific code-level findings with severity ratings. OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: You MUST produce a comprehensive report of at least 2000 characters with detailed findings, code snippets, and remediation steps for each issue.

Perform a security review of https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com. Fetch the homepage and assess: HTTPS configuration, security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options), cookie security flags, form handling, third-party script risks, and financial data protection indicators. As a financial services site, security is critical for client trust and regulatory compliance. Provide P0-P3 severity ratings with remediation steps. OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: You MUST produce a comprehensive report of at least 2000 characters with detailed findings, specific header values observed, and step-by-step remediation instructions.

Analyze https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com for performance. Fetch the homepage and examine: total page weight, asset sizes, image optimization opportunities, script loading strategy (defer/async), CSS delivery method, third-party script count and impact, caching headers, and Core Web Vitals indicators (LCP, FID, CLS). Provide specific measurable findings with targets.

Perform an SEO audit of https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com. Fetch the homepage and analyze: title tags, meta descriptions, structured data/schema markup (especially FinancialService, LocalBusiness), Open Graph tags, canonical URLs, heading hierarchy (H1-H6), internal linking structure, mobile-friendliness signals, and content quality for financial services SEO. Provide actionable recommendations ranked by impact. OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: You MUST produce a comprehensive report of at least 2000 characters with specific tag values found, missing elements, and prioritized recommendations with implementation examples.

Review https://app.jacksonheritagefinancial.com for financial services compliance and regulatory content. Fetch the homepage and key pages. Check for: SEC/FINRA disclaimer presence and accuracy, fiduciary disclosure, ADV brochure availability, privacy policy (Reg S-P), CRS (Client Relationship Summary) accessibility, fee transparency, risk disclosures, and general regulatory language. This is NOT legal advice but a content-level compliance scan. Provide P0-P3 severity findings. OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: You MUST produce a comprehensive report of at least 2000 characters with specific compliance items found or missing, exact locations, and remediation guidance.

Synthesize ALL prior specialist findings (UX, accessibility, security, performance, SEO, compliance) into a comprehensive executive report for Summit Ridge Advisors. Include: 1) Overall site health score (1-10) with justification, 2) Top 5 critical issues requiring immediate attention (P0/P1), 3) Top 5 quick wins (high impact, low effort), 4) Prioritized roadmap grouped into Sprint 1 (critical), Sprint 2 (high), Sprint 3 (medium), 5) Resource and effort estimates per sprint, 6) Financial-services-specific recommendations for trust and compliance. Format as a professional report with clear headers and tables.
